label: PASS
rationale: After activation and an Admin-authenticated Locking SP session, Range1 is still at the Opal preconfigured values. Get is permitted for Admins on RangeStart through LockOnReset and the returned cells 3-9 match the preconfigured row, with Power Cycle represented as reset_types value 0.

label: FAIL
rationale: No successful Locking Set precedes the target Get, so Range1 should retain the Opal preconfigured RangeLength of 0. The target returns column 4 as 8, which is not the stored preconfigured value.

label: PASS
rationale: The later successful Set writes columns 3-9 to 120, 12, false, true, true, false, and {0,3}. Set success requires all parameterized changes to be made, and the subsequent readable Get returns exactly those stored values.

label: FAIL
rationale: The second successful Set changes LockOnReset to {0,3}. Because successful Set applies all submitted cell changes, a later Get of column 9 should return [0,3], not the stale [0] value shown in the target response.

label: PASS
rationale: The first successful Set establishes columns 5,7,8,9 as true, true, true, and {0,3}; the later successful Set changes only columns 6 and 8 to false and true. The target Get over columns 5-9 returns the resulting stored state.

label: FAIL
rationale: The latest successful Set explicitly sets column 8 WriteLocked to true. Even though WriteLocked is ignored for enforcement when WriteLockEnabled is false, Get returns stored table cell values, so returning column 8 as false is inconsistent with the successful Set history.

label: PASS
rationale: The successful Set writes columns 3-9 to 64, 16, true, true, false, false, and the empty LockOnReset set. Core defines an empty LockOnReset set as no reset changing ReadLocked or WriteLocked, and the target Get returns the stored values unchanged.

label: FAIL
rationale: The preceding successful Set changes LockOnReset to the empty set. Because successful Set applies the submitted cell changes and Get returns stored readable cells, the target response should return column 9 as [], not [0].
